fed_stance.label: Neutral hold
fed_stance.source: CME FedWatch row for Apr 30, 2026 showed 97.9% no change and 2.1% hike.

real_yields.label: Flat
real_yields.source: FRED DFII10 showed 1.95 on 2026-04-09; no >5bp verified directional move was documented, so the factor is conservatively treated as flat.

usd_dxy.label: Weak/falling
usd_dxy.source: TradingView DXY showed 98.697, down 0.10% on Apr. 10 and down 1.48% on the week.

risk_mood.label: Balanced
risk_mood.source: Cboe VIX showed 19.23, which stays inside the 15-20 balanced band.

growth_narrative.label: Slowing
growth_narrative.source: Atlanta Fed GDPNow stood at 1.3% on Apr. 9, down from the prior 1.6% reference in the repo.

oil_supply_shock.label: Tightening
oil_supply_shock.source: EIA weekly page noted a production re-benchmarking adjustment that lowered estimated U.S. crude production by 52,000 bpd.

oil_inventories.label: Neutral
oil_inventories.source: The directly viewed EIA weekly summary confirmed the reporting week but did not cleanly expose a fresh build/draw figure in markdown, so inventories are treated neutrally instead of guessed.

gold_etf_flows.label: Supportive/inflows proxy
gold_etf_flows.source: World Gold Council commentary described gold gains driven by dip buying, dollar weakness, and softer Treasury yields, with further support expected from a renewed dollar downtrend.

credit_spreads.label: Narrowing
credit_spreads.source: FRED HY OAS showed 2.90 on 2026-04-09 versus the repository prior point of 2.94.

sox.label: Rising
sox.source: TradingView SOX showed 8,889.83, up 2.31% on Apr. 10 and up 17.83% on the week.

move_index.label: Falling
move_index.source: TradingView MOVE showed 72.1541, down 2.51% on Apr. 10 and down 20.00% over five days.

yield_curve_2s10s.label: Flattening
yield_curve_2s10s.source: FRED T10Y2Y showed 0.50 on 2026-04-10 versus the repository prior point of 0.51.

copper.label: Rising
copper.source: TradingEconomics copper showed 5.8705 USD/lb, up 2.13%, and described copper as hitting a four-week high.

ecb_stance.label: Mildly hawkish hold
ecb_stance.source: ECB decision page for 19 Mar 2026 said key rates were left unchanged, with the deposit rate at 2.00% and explicit attention to energy-driven inflation risks.

rate_diff_eur_usd.label: Stable
rate_diff_eur_usd.source: With Fed pricing still neutral and the ECB on hold rather than cutting at the latest meeting, the near-term EUR-USD policy differential is treated as stable.

eurozone_growth.label: Stable/unclear
eurozone_growth.source: No fresh official euro-area PMI release was directly captured in this browser session, so the growth input is kept neutral rather than inferred.

rba_stance.label: Hawkish/tightening
rba_stance.source: RBA March 17, 2026 release explicitly raised the cash rate target by 25 bps to 4.10%.

rate_diff_aud_usd.label: Widening vs USD
rate_diff_aud_usd.source: The March RBA hike versus a neutral Fed improves the AUD side of the short-rate differential.

risk_sentiment_aud.label: Neutral
risk_sentiment_aud.source: The broad risk backdrop is not risk-on enough for +1 and not risk-off enough for -1 because VIX is in the balanced 15-20 band.

china_growth.label: Accelerating
china_growth.source: Official NBS release showed March manufacturing PMI at 50.4, up from 49.0 and back above 50.

boj_stance.label: Hold
boj_stance.source: BoJ March 19, 2026 statement kept the overnight call rate around 0.75% by an 8-1 vote.

rate_diff_jpy_usd.label: Improving for JPY
rate_diff_jpy_usd.source: A neutral Fed plus BoJ normalization keeps the policy-divergence story favorable for JPY futures.

geopolitical_risk.label: Stable
geopolitical_risk.source: No directly verified same-session escalation metric was collected beyond broader ceasefire fragility, so the factor is treated as stable.
